frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: decode TMS parameters from spatial BOLD patterns at stimulation site

frameworks.name: concurrent TMS-fMRI
frameworks.one_line: simultaneous stimulation and BOLD readout to assay local neural effects

core_question: Does TMS alter neural activity at the stimulation site even when overall BOLD signal is unchanged?
key_assumptions: multivoxel BOLD patterns index population-level neural state
motor threshold provides a meaningful intensity reference for DLPFC stimulation
manipulations: TMS intensity (100% vs 50% of motor threshold)
TMS frequency (5, 8.33, 12.5, 25 Hz) at fixed high intensity
stimulation site fixed at dorsolateral prefrontal cortex
key_findings: TMS intensity reliably decoded from multivoxel patterns at the stimulation site
TMS frequency reliably decoded from multivoxel patterns at the stimulation site
overall BOLD activity at the site of stimulation was unaffected by TMS in both experiments
interpretations: TMS produces detectable local neural effects that are invisible to univariate BOLD analyses
multivariate techniques can clarify TMS's precise effects on neural activity
